oneLineSummary: A salon-focused digital experience concept exploring service presentation, booking touchpoints, and a more polished brand-led customer journey.

summary.systemDefinition: A service-oriented frontend concept for a salon booking and brand experience.
coreWorkflowsFeatures: Brand-led visual hierarchy and trust-building details
Responsive screens for customer decision points
summary.businessOrOperationalImpact: The concept explores how service presentation and booking clarity can strengthen customer confidence before conversion.
summary.engineeringValue: It highlights frontend hierarchy decisions, service design thinking, and polished interaction planning.

projectNarrative.problem: Service businesses need digital experiences that build trust quickly while keeping booking journeys simple.
projectNarrative.approach: I designed a salon-focused experience that balances visual polish, service explanation, and clear customer action points.
projectNarrative.result: The case study demonstrates how interface hierarchy can support both branding and usability.

engineeringHighlights: Service design translated into responsive UI structure
Visual hierarchy tuned for trust and conversion touchpoints
Case-study based exploration of branded UX decisions

reflection.tradeoffs: Creating a polished branded feel required careful restraint so the interface remained scannable and booking-focused.
lessonsLearned: Visual polish is most effective when it supports decision clarity.
Service interfaces need explicit trust signals.
Responsive refinement often determines whether a concept feels practical.

display.libraryFocus: visual hierarchy, trust-building details, and service booking usability
